fixes.description: Flip IPI1 -> IPA1 edge from -1 (inhibition) to +1 (activation)
fixes.reason: T058 (IPI1 KO) and T059 (IPI1 OE) fail with opposite-sign predictions; paper states IPI1 stabilises IPA1 in shoot apex (not degrades)
fixes.biological_justification: IPI1 RING E3 ligase is tissue-specific: in shoot apex it stabilises OsSPL14/IPA1 via K63-linked polyubiquitination, while in panicle it may promote degradation. ipi1-CRISPR mutants show reduced IPA1 and increased tillers, consistent with the +1 sign.

fixes.description: Add HK4 -> TB1 (-1) representing direct CK receptor repression of OsTB1
fixes.reason: T039 (HK4 KO), T152 (ohk4_ipa1), T200 (OsHK3_OsHK4) all fail because HK4 KO only propagates through HK4->AHP->RR21->IPA1->TB1(+1) giving TB1 DOWN, when biology requires TB1 UP
fixes.biological_justification: Chun et al. 2023 showed ohk4 exhibits reduced tillering with de-repressed OsTB1, mediated by CK-receptor-phosphorelay-activated B-type response regulators binding and repressing the OsTB1 promoter in axillary meristems.

fixes.description: Re-encode T075 DST-reg1 from gm=2.0 (GOF) to gm=0.5 (semi-dominant LOF)
fixes.reason: T075 fails at ratio 0.725; the reg1 allele is a semi-dominant LOF that REDUCES DST binding to OsCKX2 promoter (not a canonical GOF of DST function), producing reduced CKX2 and elevated CK.
fixes.biological_justification: Li et al. 2013 PNAS characterised reg1 as a missense allele with reduced DNA-binding, leading to decreased CKX2 transcription, higher CK, and modestly increased tillers. The correct encoding is a knockdown of DST function (gm=0.5).

results.reason: Best-method accuracy 75.2% -> 77.0% (+1.8%); 5 tests fixed (T039,T058,T059,T075,T200), 2 broken (T096,T169), net +3.
